verses.text: He pleads, 'Judge me, O God, and plead my cause' against the ungodly, asking why God seems to cast him off. He longs for vindication. The wronged soul appeals to God as both advocate and judge.
He pleads, 'Judge me, O God, and plead my cause' against the ungodly, asking why God seems to cast him off. He longs for vindication. The wronged soul appeals to God as both advocate and judge.

'O send out thy light and thy truth: let them lead me' to God's holy hill and altar, his exceeding joy. He longs to return to worship. God's light and truth are the guides that bring the soul home to Himself.

'O send out thy light and thy truth: let them lead me' to God's holy hill and altar, his exceeding joy. He longs to return to worship. God's light and truth are the guides that bring the soul home to Himself.
Once more: 'Why art thou cast down, O my soul?... hope in God: for I shall yet praise him.' The refrain steadies him. The way out of despondency is to fix hope on the God who will yet be our praise.
